framework.bundler: Webpack or Vite with CSS Modules (content-hashed class names)
framework.not_nextjs: #__next element does not exist; NavigatorClientEntry.tsx suggests custom SPA bootstrap, not Next.js app router

api_endpoint.cors_preflight: OPTIONS to same URL precedes the POST (visible as duplicate rows in Network)

request_payload_fields_observed.content: string — the user prompt text verbatim
request_payload_fields_observed.references: [] (empty array in baseline; used when @ mentions attach repos/files)
request_payload_fields_observed.structure_note: Pure JSON POST body with threading + skill system fields. Same endpoint used for both first and subsequent messages in a thread.

sse_schema.format: classic SSE (data: <json>\n\n) — NO event: lines (which is why Chrome DevTools EventStream tab renders empty)
sse_schema.chunks_per_response: 1 content event per chunk (for short responses) + 1 complete event at end

content.delta_semantics: body is cumulative text since last content event; client appends to the displayed bubble

complete.notes: model and turnId are empty strings in observed responses. createdAt is RFC3339 nanosecond-precision. copilotAnnotations is GitHub's code-safety annotation block (always empty for non-code responses).

sse_schema.capture_method: window.fetch override (fetch-patch.ts DID respect the override — unlike Google's, GitHub's internal fetch wrapper uses live window.fetch at call time, so our interceptor ran and ReadableStream.getReader() consumed the body via response.clone()).
sse_schema.compared_to_deepseek_451: SIMPLER. DeepSeek uses named events (ready/update_session/title/close) + {p,o,v} JSON-Patch ops with path inheritance. Copilot uses only `data:` with 2 event-type-tagged JSON objects. MUCH easier to replicate for APF envelope injection.

thinking_ui.observation: For claude-haiku-4.5 responses (the account default), responses arrive so fast (< 2s) that the thinking/loading UI is barely visible. A typing animation may flash briefly during the first content event.
thinking_ui.no_dedicated_screenshot: 08_thinking.png captured post-response because response was rendered before screenshot fired
thinking_ui.dom_selector: not isolated — subsumed by ChatMessage-module__chatMessage_* container

error_ui_observed.method_used: Console fetch override returning synthesized Response with status=500 and empty body, gated on url match for '/messages'
error_ui_observed.method_1_offline_toggle: not run (fetch override was more surgical and sufficient)
error_ui_observed.result: Inline primer-react Banner rendered below the user message with text: 'I'm sorry but there was an error. Please try again.' + warning icon + retry button
error_ui_observed.text_source: STATIC i18n string — NOT read from response body (confirmed by returning empty body → generic text appeared)

dom_selector.description: div.BannerDescription (static class) nested under div.prc-Banner-BannerContent-LraS2 under div.prc-Banner-BannerContainer-T-S*
dom_selector.class_hash_stability: BannerDescription is a static class; outer hashes (LraS2, T-S) change with each deployment

stale_infrastructure_present.js-stale-session-flash: exists in DOM (div#.js-stale-session-flash stale-session-fla...) — GitHub's legacy session expiry flash, unused by Copilot error path
stale_infrastructure_present.ajax-error-message: div#ajax-error-message.ajax-error-message flash flash-error — EMPTY text, GitHub's legacy XHR error flash, unused by Copilot error path
stale_infrastructure_present.note: GitHub's legacy .js-flash / .flash-error infrastructure is PRESENT in the page shell (as noted in early DIAG: flashEls=2) but Copilot Chat uses its OWN error display via primer-react Banner component, not the legacy flash elements.

hidden_error_hooks: div.BannerDescription (Copilot's error message text node — STATIC i18n content)
div.prc-Banner-BannerContent-LraS2 (primer-react Banner content wrapper — hashed class)
div.prc-Banner-BannerContainer-T-S* (primer-react Banner outer container — hashed class)
div#.js-stale-session-flash (legacy GitHub flash, UNUSED by Copilot)
div#ajax-error-message.ajax-error-message.flash.flash-error (legacy GitHub XHR error flash, UNUSED by Copilot)

chat_bubble_dom.class_naming_convention: CSS Modules — `<ModuleName>-module__<className>__<contentHash>` (e.g. ChatMessage-module__content__haI0H). Hashes rotate on each build.
chat_bubble_dom.framework: primer-react (for Banner, Buttons) + custom React components with CSS Modules (for ChatMessage / ImmersiveChat) + react-router for routing
chat_bubble_dom.markdown_rendering: Copilot renders markdown as HTML inside message-container — includes fenced code blocks (with dedicated `View file: X.py` button for code), ordered/unordered lists (rendered as <li> with bullet/number), and syntax-highlighted `<code>` spans. Feedback row (Good response / Bad response / Copy / Retry with Claude Haiku 4.5 / Retry with model) appears as primer-react IconButton row below each assistant message.

csp_header.verdict: HOST-BASED allowlisting. Only github.githubassets.com can serve scripts. Any injected <script> tag from github.com or inline JS is blocked. Non-script DOM additions (text, divs, spans) are NOT constrained by script-src. Since APF operates at the HTTP layer, CSP is largely IRRELEVANT to envelope/body replacement — the browser trusts what APF serves because APF IS the network transport.
csp_header.comparison: Less strict than Gemini #452 (nonce + strict-dynamic) but still blocks inline script. Same overall shape as v0 #447.

a_sse_stream_injection.rationale: GitHub Copilot's response IS text/event-stream with a simple 2-event-type schema (content + complete). APF can substitute the response body with synthesized content+complete events containing the warning text as body. React app will render as a normal assistant bubble indistinguishable from a real response.
a_sse_stream_injection.parent_message_id_source: Extract from request JSON body field 'responseMessageID' — use the SAME value in the response complete event's id OR parentMessageID. Using responseMessageID directly as complete.id matches what the client expected for that in-flight message.
a_sse_stream_injection.caveats: If parentMessageID doesn't match what client expects, the React app may not append the bubble to the thread (needs validation in staging). A lenient approach: generate new random UUIDs for id and use request's parentMessageID for parentMessageID.
a_sse_stream_injection.markdown_support: body text supports markdown (renders to HTML, code blocks, lists) — APF can use this for richer warning formatting.

b_http_body_html_replacement.rationale: Copilot's error path does NOT read response body text — Banner shows static 'I'm sorry but there was an error. Please try again.' regardless of body contents. Returning HTML as a 500 body is ignored by the React app's error handler.
b_http_body_html_replacement.test_result: Empty 500 body → generic banner text. No body parsing observed.

c_js_error_banner_populate.rationale: GitHub's legacy .js-flash / .flash-error elements exist in the page shell BUT are not wired to Copilot's chat flow. Copilot's error banner is a React-rendered primer-react Banner with HARDCODED i18n text. The React error state doesn't accept an external message string — the text is imported from a locale bundle at compile time.
c_js_error_banner_populate.dom_evidence: Banner text is from `div.BannerDescription` which is populated by React on render, not by mutation. The parent container class is `prc-Banner-BannerContent-LraS2` (hashed).

d_dom_direct_inject.rationale: APF can modify the main document GET response for `github.com/copilot` to inject a banner <div> before React mounts. Once React hydrates and React Router takes over, our injected div will be REMOVED when React re-renders the root container. Also: since Copilot chat is React Router CSR, subsequent /copilot/c/{uuid} navigations happen client-side and never go through APF for the document fetch, so the warning only shows on initial load. For a first-time session warning, this is acceptable; for repeated warning, NO.
d_dom_direct_inject.csp_consideration: Non-script DOM (text, divs, spans) is NOT constrained by script-src; APF can safely add markup. But style-src may be host-restricted — use inline style attributes sparingly or include pre-approved utility classes.

e_block_page_substitution.verdict: BLOCKED (intra-session), POSSIBLE (initial load only — same constraints as v0 #447)
e_block_page_substitution.rationale: Copilot is a pure CSR React Router SPA. After initial load, all routing is client-side via pushState. APF never sees the subsequent navigation requests for the document HTML (React just updates component state). Replacing the initial /copilot HTML with a block page works only on the very first request. After that, any attempt to substitute triggers on non-document requests and breaks the SPA.

recommended_option.primary_rationale: The ONLY option that reliably delivers a user-visible warning bubble on EVERY Copilot chat interaction, without page reload, without breaking the SPA. Schema is simpler than DeepSeek's JSON-Patch format — just 2 event types. APF already handles SSE envelope replacement for DeepSeek; extending to Copilot requires a new envelope template but shares the same transport layer.
recommended_option.secondary: Option D (initial-load banner) — for first-time session disclaimer (e.g., 'Reminder: you are subject to corporate usage policy'). Combine with Option A for chat-level warnings.
recommended_option.skip: Options B, C, E are blocked or only marginally viable and should NOT be implemented.
implementation_notes: Trigger condition: body.content matches any APF_SENSITIVE pattern after PROMPT inspection
Replacement: synthesize SSE body per envelope_template above; set Content-Type: text/event-stream; Status: 200
parentMessageID extraction: parse request JSON body, read .responseMessageID field — use it as .parentMessageID in the complete event (ensures threading continuity)
id generation: crypto.randomUUID() for the complete.id field
createdAt: new Date().toISOString() — Copilot accepts ISO8601 with milliseconds; nanosecond precision (as observed) is not required

CORS: api.individual.githubcopilot.com is a separate origin from github.com; APF must preserve Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://github.com and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true headers on the replaced response (copy from original upstream) OR the browser will reject the response.

phase_e_console_and_telemetry.likely_cause: react-router development build console warnings (non-actionable, NOT a GitHub Copilot error)

telemetry_endpoints_observed.purpose: GitHub Analytics pixel (equivalent to Google's gen_204)
Client-side JS error reporting (GitHub's equivalent of Gemini's jserror pipeline)

phase_e_console_and_telemetry.console_scroll_snapshot: results/files/453/06_console_error.png + results/files/453/04_console_diag.png — visible console stack including chunk-EPOLDU6W.mjs:193 (react-router) and NavigatorClientEntry.tsx:84

comparative_notes.vs_447_v0: Same React Router pushState pattern. Same Option E blocked conclusion. v0 had NO error UI at all on transport failures; Copilot has a STATIC-TEXT primer-react Banner. Both share CSR SPA constraint.
comparative_notes.vs_448_v0_navigation: Same is_document_request=FALSE observation (Copilot SPA never re-fetches document).
comparative_notes.vs_449_gamma: Different — gamma had empty body due to APF bootstrap failure. Copilot body hydrates fully (21KB HTML shell). Copilot's initial DIAG showing body.innerText=15 was pre-hydration timing artifact, NOT a bootstrap failure.
comparative_notes.vs_451_deepseek: Both use SSE with 200 status. Copilot's schema is SIMPLER (2 event types, flat JSON) vs DeepSeek ({p,o,v} JSON-Patch with path inheritance). Copilot Option A is EASIER to implement than DeepSeek Option A.
comparative_notes.vs_452_gemini3: Gemini has strict nonce+strict-dynamic CSP blocking ALL DOM inject; Copilot has HOST-BASED CSP which is less strict but still blocks inline script. Gemini silent-fails without user-visible error; Copilot shows a GENERIC error banner (better UX, but still no way to inject our text into the banner). Gemini's wrb.fr envelope is the ONLY path; Copilot has BOTH Option A (preferred) AND Option D (initial load).
comparative_notes.apf_pipeline_impact: Copilot joins DeepSeek in the 'Option A works cleanly' bucket. v0, Gemini, gamma remain in the 'special-case required' bucket. Recommend prioritizing Copilot envelope implementation next (easier than DeepSeek's JSON-Patch) to quickly bring 2 services to parity.

notes: Complete multi-phase probe executed in one extended session across multiple /loop cycles and one mid-session context-checkpoint (memory-pinned for /compact safety). Phase A SSE body was captured via a window.fetch interceptor (GitHub's fetch-patch.ts respects the live window.fetch reference, unlike Google's), which ALSO worked for Phase B error injection via the same override. No actual DevTools Offline toggle or Block URL was needed — the fetch override delivered equivalent signal and was more surgical. The primer-react Banner component with static i18n text is the CRITICAL finding that BLOCKS Options B and C. The SSE schema simplicity (2 event types, flat JSON) makes Option A the clear winner for APF implementation. Expected time ~10-15 min per request spec; actual time ~40 min due to pre-hydration DIAG false-positive + mid-session context checkpoint + DevTools Response tab limitations requiring fetch override fallback. Screenshot 10_messages_headers.png was discarded (captured Claude Code window after Chrome lost focus) and NOT included in the final screenshot list.
